subject: Computer Networking
title: How Radio Waves Carry Data
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Radio Waves Carry Data.

hook.text: Your phone has no wire to the internet. Data travels on invisible radio waves through the air.

buildup.text: Radio waves are electromagnetic energy. A transmitter encodes data by varying the wave's frequency or amplitude.

discovery.text: A receiver decodes those variations back into digital bits. Modulation turns ones and zeros into wave patterns.

twist.text: Radio waves weaken with distance and get blocked by walls, rain, and other signals. Wireless is inherently unreliable.

climax.text: Error correction codes add redundancy so the receiver can reconstruct damaged data without retransmission.

punchline.text: Wireless data is just information riding invisible waves.

quiz.question: How is data encoded onto radio waves?
options: By modulating the wave's frequency or amplitude
By changing the color of the radio signal
By increasing the physical size of the antenna
